description:
You have just joined a DevOps team as the container-environment specialist.  
    Your first assignment is to standardise the runtime configuration for three
    micro-services that will eventually be orchestrated in containers.

    Summary of what must exist when you are done
    ============================================
    1. Directory structure under /home/user/microservices containing
       • /home/user/microservices/auth
       • /home/user/microservices/billing
       • /home/user/microservices/gateway

    2. One “.env” file per service, **exactly** named “.env” and placed in the
       corresponding service directory.  These files must contain **only**
       the key=value pairs listed below, one per line, with no extra spaces
       before or after the keys, values or the equal sign.

       2.1 /home/user/microservices/auth/.env  
           DB_HOST=auth-db  
           DB_PORT=5432  
           JWT_SECRET=SuperSecretAuth  
           REDIS_URL=redis://cache:6379/0  

       2.2 /home/user/microservices/billing/.env  
           DB_HOST=billing-db  
           DB_PORT=3306  
           PAYMENT_GATEWAY_KEY=sk_test_ABC123  
           CURRENCY=USD  

       2.3 /home/user/microservices/gateway/.env  
           API_HOST=0.0.0.0  
           API_PORT=8080  
           AUTH_SERVICE_URL=http://auth:5000  
           BILLING_SERVICE_URL=http://billing:6000  
           LOG_LEVEL=INFO  

    3. A CSV summary file called  
       /home/user/microservices/config_summary.log

       The file must contain **exactly** four lines, using UNIX line-endings
       (`\n`).  The first line is a header.  The next three lines are the
       summary lines for the individual services, listed in
       alphabetical order of the service names (auth, billing, gateway).

       Format (comma-separated, **no spaces** anywhere):
           service,variable_count,first_variable,last_variable

       Required final contents of the file:

           service,variable_count,first_variable,last_variable
           auth,4,DB_HOST,REDIS_URL
           billing,4,CURRENCY,PAYMENT_GATEWAY_KEY
           gateway,5,API_HOST,LOG_LEVEL

       • variable_count is the total number of environment variables
         in that service’s “.env” file.
       • first_variable and last_variable are the first and last key names
         when the keys are sorted alphabetically.

    4. When everything is complete, display the contents of
       /home/user/microservices/config_summary.log to the terminal so that the
       tester can capture the output.

    Practical constraints
    ---------------------
    • All work must be performed as the normal user inside /home/user.  
    • You do NOT have root or sudo privileges.  
    • The whole task can be achieved with 5–10 shell commands.  
    • Do not install additional software; make use of standard GNU/Linux
      utilities that are already present (e.g., mkdir, printf, cat, etc.).  

    The automated tester will verify:
    • Existence of the three directories.  
    • Exact byte-for-byte contents of the three “.env” files.  
    • Exact byte-for-byte contents of /home/user/microservices/config_summary.log.  
    • That the last command you issue prints the summary file’s contents to
      STDOUT, matching the required four-line CSV shown above.

    Hint: build the “.env” files first, then generate the summary with simple
    text-processing commands, redirect it to config_summary.log, and finally
    `cat` the log to the console.
truth:
Expected artefacts created by the agent
    ---------------------------------------

    Directories:
      /home/user/microservices
      /home/user/microservices/auth
      /home/user/microservices/billing
      /home/user/microservices/gateway

    Files and their exact contents (UNIX line endings):

    1. /home/user/microservices/auth/.env
       ------------------------------------------------
       DB_HOST=auth-db
       DB_PORT=5432
       JWT_SECRET=SuperSecretAuth
       REDIS_URL=redis://cache:6379/0
       ------------------------------------------------

    2. /home/user/microservices/billing/.env
       ------------------------------------------------
       DB_HOST=billing-db
       DB_PORT=3306
       PAYMENT_GATEWAY_KEY=sk_test_ABC123
       CURRENCY=USD
       ------------------------------------------------

    3. /home/user/microservices/gateway/.env
       ------------------------------------------------
       API_HOST=0.0.0.0
       API_PORT=8080
       AUTH_SERVICE_URL=http://auth:5000
       BILLING_SERVICE_URL=http://billing:6000
       LOG_LEVEL=INFO
       ------------------------------------------------

    4. /home/user/microservices/config_summary.log
       ------------------------------------------------
       service,variable_count,first_variable,last_variable
       auth,4,DB_HOST,REDIS_URL
       billing,4,CURRENCY,PAYMENT_GATEWAY_KEY
       gateway,5,API_HOST,LOG_LEVEL
       ------------------------------------------------
